channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas frees 4 female Israeli soldiers as part of the Gaza ceasefire in exchange for 200 prisoners
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas militants handed over four captive female Israeli soldiers to the Red Cross in Gaza City on Saturday after parading them in front of a crowd. Israel followed with the release of 200 Palestinian prisoners or detainees as part of the fragile ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's 5 biggest moves on immigration so far
item.description: President Trump released a flurry of immigration actions this week, signing a series of orders targeting the border and ramping up enforcement. While Trump has long pledged to address illegal immigration, many of his actions targeted longstanding legal pathways.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Hamas releases 4 female hostages as part of Israel ceasefire deal
item.description: The terrorist group Hamas released four more hostages from Gaza on Saturday under a ceasefire deal with Israel that took effect nearly a week ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Auschwitz was liberated 80 years ago. The spotlight is on survivors as their numbers dwindle
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The world's focus will be on the remaining survivors of Nazi Germany's atrocities on Monday as world leaders and royalty join them for commemorations on the 80th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippines accuses China's forces of harassing fisheries vessels in the South China Sea
item.description: The Philippine coast guard says Chinese coast guard ships and a Chinese navy helicopter harassed a group of Philippine fisheries vessels conducting a scientific survey in a hotly disputed area of the South China Sea, forcing them to cancel the operation.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House Democrats urge Rubio to lift foreign assistance freeze
item.description: House Democrats urged President Trump’s Secretary of State Marco Rubio to lift the foreign assistance freeze mandated by an executive order from earlier this week, cutting off most U.S-funded foreign development projects around the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: South Koreans turn funeral wreaths and K-pop light sticks into political protest tools
item.description: South Korean protesters are repurposing traditional flower wreaths and K-pop light sticks as political protest tools amid President Yoon Suk Yeol’s impeachment trial, with both supporters and opponents sending hundreds of wreaths to government buildings since his December martial law declaration.
item.dc:creator: Juwon Park, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fighting in Sudan's civil war sets ablaze the country's largest oil refinery, satellite photos show
item.description: Fighting around Sudan’s largest oil refinery has set the sprawling complex ablaze, sending thick, black smoke over the country’s capital, Khartoum.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Colombia president decrees emergency powers to restore order in coca region wracked by rebel combat
item.description: Colombia's President Gustavo Petro has issued a decree giving him emergency powers to restore order in Catatumbo, a coca-growing region wracked by a deadly turf war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump had ‘fiery’ call with Danish prime minister over Greenland: Report
item.description: President Trump reportedly held a “fiery” call with Denmark’s Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen over the president’s insistence that U.S. control of Greenland is necessary for American national security.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hamas releases names of 4 female hostages to be released next, possibly violates deal
item.description: Hamas says it will release four female IDF soldiers including Karina Ariev, Daniella Gilboa, Naama Levy and Liri Albag in second hostage release in a possible violation of the ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu suggests Israel might not complete its withdrawal from Lebanon by a ceasefire deadline
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu suggested Friday that Israel might not withdraw all of its forces from Lebanon by a deadline set in its ceasefire with Hezbollah, and Washington appears prepared to push for an extension.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Major storm lashes Ireland and Scotland as hurricane-force winds down power lines and ground flights
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Millions of people in Ireland and northern parts of the U.K. heeded the advice of authorities to stay at home Friday in the face of hurricane-force winds that disabled power networks and brought widespread travel disruptions.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of thousands protest in Slovakia over pro-Russia policies of populist leader Fico
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people in Slovakia gathered at squares and street across the country on Friday to voice their opposition to the pro-Russia policies of populist Prime Minister Robert Fico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Governor of Congo's North Kivu province dies of his wounds in fighting with rebels, authorities say
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — The governor of eastern Congo’s North Kivu province has died from injuries sustained during fighting on the front line, a military spokesman said Friday, as M23 rebels closed in on the provincial capital.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New pandas make their debut at National Zoo
item.description: The National Zoo announced pandas have returned to Washington and made their public debut Friday. “The wait is OVER: the giant pandas have officially made their public debut at the Smithsonian’s National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: China's Lunar New Year travel rush kicks into high gear with 9 billion trips expected
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China’s Lunar New Year travel rush has kicked into high gear, with billions of trips expected in coming days for the peak of the 40-day annual mass migration — the world's biggest annual movement of humanity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea prepares to send more troops to Russia after suffering casualties, South Korea says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s military said Friday it suspects North Korea is preparing to send additional troops to Russia after its soldiers fighting in the Russian-Ukraine war suffered heavy casualties.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taliban reject court move to arrest top officials for persecuting Afghan women and girls
item.description: The Taliban Friday rejected a court move to arrest two of their top officials for persecuting women, accusing the court of baseless accusations and misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's leader says he's preparing for tough negotiations with Trump on maintaining US presence
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, struggling to stabilize his minority government, said Friday he will seek to maintain regional security and prepare for tough negotiations with U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rubio set to travel to Panama on first trip
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is traveling to Panama late next week in his first official diplomatic trip late, amid heightened tensions over President Trump’s threats to retake the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump: Make products in America or face tariffs
item.description: “Come make your product in America, and we will give you among the lowest taxes of any nation on earth,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Survivor of Nova music festival Hamas terror attack wins slot to represent Israel at Eurovision
item.description: Yuval Raphael, survivor of the Nova music festival terrorist attack by Hamas in October 2023, has turned to music to help her process her trauma and will represent Israel in the Eurovision contest.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kremlin sees nothing 'new' in Trump's tariff threats over Ukraine war
item.description: The Kremlin is unmoved by President Trump’s threats to impose sanctions if Russia does not bow out of its conflict with Ukraine. “We don't see any particularly new elements here.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump picks former fast-food chain CEO Andrew Puzder for EU ambassador
item.description: President Trump has selected Andrew Puzder, a former fast-food chain CEO who he attempted to install in his first White House Cabinet, as his pick for ambassador to the European Union. Trump praised Puzder, the former chief of CKE restaurants, as steering fast-food chains like Carl’s Jr.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Saudi crown prince says kingdom intends to invest $600 billion in US during call with Trump
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s crown prince says the kingdom wants to invest $600 billion in the United States over the next four years, comments that came after President Donald Trump mused about returning to the kingdom as his first foreign trip.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2 people are killed in a knife attack in Germany; Scholz says there must be consequences
item.description: A stabbing attack in Germany has left two people dead, including a 2-year-old boy, and three others injured; the suspect was arrested shortly after the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House cheers release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden, Qatar
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JON GAMBRELL and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Putin, Xi vow to ‘deepen’ alliance hours after Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin held a video call hours after President Donald Trump re-entered the White House and pledged to "deepen" their partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Russia sounds off on Trump’s threat to retake the Panama Canal
item.description: Russia’s foreign ministry has called on Trump to reaffirm the current international agreement surrounding the Panama Canal and to leave it in control of the nation of Panama.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
